method_atom: an accounting framework for stochastic systems steered by a feedback loop, quantifying the reduction in configurational uncertainty (entropy) produced by repeatedly measuring the system's state and conditioning subsequent actions on the outcome, and using this information-gain term to close the balance that bounds the maximum resource (work) extractable when control is conditioned on acquired information.
object_atom: isothermal feedback-controlled physical systems, in particular Maxwell's-demon-type setups, exemplified by a simple information-fueled particle-pumping device operating at fixed temperature.
brief_reason: the entropy-reduction-from-repeated-measurement-and-conditioned-action bookkeeping and its resulting extractable-work bound are the transferable method, while the isothermal thermal engine / Maxwell's demon / particle pump are the physics-specific objects.
